twitter_thread: 70% of my 17 Cloudflare Workers were redundant due to poor routing, a $10k mistake.
Cloudflare Worker caching reduced latency by 32% in our e-commerce platform.
CPU-intensive tasks on Workers cost us $500/month, switched to dedicated servers.
A/B testing with Workers increased conversions by 12% for our SaaS product.
Worker memory usage crashed our app 3 times, now we monitor it daily.
Custom auth flows with Workers saved us 2 months of dev time.
What's the most surprising Cloudflare Worker use case you've encountered? #cloudflare #workers
pack.linkedin: After building 17 Cloudflare Workers, I found that 70% were redundant, a $10k mistake. Proper routing design and caching reduced latency by 32%. We also learned to avoid CPU-intensive tasks, which cost $500/month. A/B testing with Workers increased conversions by 12%. Monitoring memory usage is crucial, as crashes can be devastating. How have you used Cloudflare Workers to improve your application's performance?
tiktok_hooks: I wasted $10k on redundant Cloudflare Workers, here's what I learned
How I reduced latency by 32% with one simple Cloudflare Worker tweak
The hidden cost of using Cloudflare Workers for CPU-intensive tasks
pack.reddit_headline: I built 17 Cloudflare Workers and learned a costly lesson: are they worth it?
